skill.name: biogrid-orcs
skill.description: Accesses BioGRID ORCS CRISPR screen data (organisms, screens, scores). Invoke when user needs to search CRISPR screens, get vocabulary, or retrieve gene scores.
skill.summary: Query CRISPR screen data from the BioGRID ORCS database using a Python API client
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate API client for the BioGRID ORCS CRISPR screen database (orcsws.thebiogrid.org). All 86 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are false positives caused by the static scanner misinterpreting markdown code formatting backticks as Ruby shell execution syntax. All 5 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are false positives — no cryptographic operations exist in the codebase. The only confirmed risks are network access to a legitimate scientific API and Python script execution, both of which are expected and documented behavior for this skill.

low_findings.title: Network access to external API
low_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP requests to the BioGRID ORCS REST API at orcsws.thebiogrid.org. This is expected and documented behavior for a scientific data retrieval tool. The URL is a hardcoded legitimate scientific database endpoint, and the access key is user-provided at runtime.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in documentation
low_findings.description: Documentation and source code contain hardcoded references to the BioGRID ORCS API base URL. This is expected for an API client skill and points to a legitimate scientific database.

content.user_title: Query BioGRID ORCS CRISPR Screen Data
content.value_statement: Researchers need a simple way to search and retrieve CRISPR screen data from the BioGRID ORCS database. This skill provides a command-line client to query organisms, screens, scores, and gene data through the official REST API with structured output.
actual_capabilities: List supported organisms available in the BioGRID ORCS database
Search and filter CRISPR screens by cell line, organism, phenotype, and other criteria
Retrieve gene scores across all CRISPR screens with hit significance filtering
Access controlled vocabulary terms used to classify screens in the database
Query multiple genes simultaneously using pipe-separated identifiers
limitations: Requires a valid BioGRID access key to authenticate API requests
Cannot create, modify, or delete data in the BioGRID ORCS database
Limited to data fields and filters exposed by the official ORCS REST API
Placeholder values in example commands must be replaced with real identifiers before execution

use_cases.title: Search CRISPR screens by cell line and organism
use_cases.description: A biologist searches for CRISPR screens conducted on HeLa cells in human samples to find relevant studies for their experiment design.

use_cases.title: Retrieve gene scores for validation
use_cases.description: A researcher validates candidate genes by checking their scores across all published CRISPR screens in the BioGRID ORCS database.

use_cases.title: Explore available organisms and vocabulary
use_cases.description: A new user explores the organisms and controlled vocabulary available in BioGRID ORCS before designing specific queries.

prompt_templates.title: List all available organisms
prompt_templates.prompt: List all organisms available in the BioGRID ORCS database using the organisms command.

prompt_templates.title: Find screens for a specific gene and cell line
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for CRISPR screens in human cells (organismID 9606) that used HeLa cell line and target BRCA1.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user searching for relevant screens

prompt_templates.title: Retrieve gene scores with hit filtering
prompt_templates.prompt: Get all screen scores for gene TP53 and show only results marked as significant hits.

prompt_templates.title: Batch query multiple genes
prompt_templates.prompt: Search gene scores for BRCA1 and TP53 using the genes command, filter by human organism and show only significant hits.

output_examples.input: List all organisms in BioGRID ORCS
output_examples.output: A formatted table showing organism ID, scientific name, and common name for each organism in the database (e.g., 9606 - Homo sapiens - Human, 10090 - Mus musculus - Mouse)

output_examples.input: Get gene scores for TP53 across all screens
output_examples.output: A summary report listing each screen ID where TP53 was tested, its score value, hit significance flag, cell line, and publication PubMed ID

best_practices: Always provide a valid access key using the --accesskey argument before running any query
Start by listing organisms or vocabulary to understand available filters and identifiers before running complex queries
Use JSON output format for programmatic processing of results and tab format for human-readable tables
anti_patterns: Running commands without specifying a valid BioGRID access key will fail with an authentication error
Attempting to use the skill for data outside the scope of CRISPR screen data from BioGRID ORCS will produce irrelevant results
Using the skill without first confirming the required organism IDs or screen identifiers will lead to failed queries

faq.question: What is BioGRID ORCS?
faq.answer: BioGRID ORCS is a database of CRISPR screen results maintained by the BioGRID project. It stores data from published CRISPR screens including gene scores and experimental metadata.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a valid BioGRID access key to authenticate API requests. You can obtain one from the BioGRID website.

faq.question: What types of data can I retrieve?
faq.answer: You can retrieve organisms, CRISPR screens, gene scores across screens, and controlled vocabulary terms from the BioGRID ORCS database.

faq.question: Can I filter screen results by specific criteria?
faq.answer: Yes, you can filter by cell line, organism ID, phenotype, screen type, throughput, enzyme, and many other experimental parameters.

faq.question: What output formats does the skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports JSON format for structured data processing and tab-delimited format for human-readable tables.

faq.question: Is this an official BioGRID tool?
faq.answer: This is a community-developed skill that uses the official BioGRID ORCS REST API. It is not an official BioGRID project.
